research.summary: Plumber GBP rankings are dominated by Google Business Profile signals (~32% of weight per Whitespark 2026) + review velocity (Google now weighs the most recent 90 days more heavily than lifetime totals). A business with 200 reviews and none in the past 6 months ranks BELOW a business with 80 reviews and steady weekly flow. Category match is straightforward in this vertical — 'Plumber' is the canonical primary; no ambiguity like garage-door has.
research.recommendedPrimaryCategoryReasoning: Empirically dominant in Culver City top-5 (8/8 leads use 'Plumber'). Canonical GBP category, no broader/narrower ambiguity to navigate.

topRankingFactors.benchmark: Single most important. Verified address must be in the city you target.
topRankingFactors.source: Emulent Weighted Local SEO Ranking Factors 2026

topRankingFactors.benchmark: Primary category exact match + all service attributes filled + complete hours/photos/posts.
Target 20-25 new reviews/month. Recency outweighs lifetime totals in 2026.
Submit to 50+ core directories. Identical NAP everywhere.
≥4.6 avg rating, ≥50 reviews to compete in top 3.
Service+city in title and h1, LocalBusiness schema, <2.5s load.

commonPitfalls: Stale GBP posts (>90 days since last post — Google reads as dormant)
200+ reviews but none in last 6 months (recency penalty)
Multiple call-tracker numbers on the website breaking NAP
Generic CTAs ('Contact Us') instead of action-specific ('Call Now for Same-Day Service')
Missing 'Emergency' or '24/7' attributes when those services are offered
Service area set too wide (decay penalty for being far from searchers)
research.titleTagExample: Plumber in Culver City, CA | Pacific Plumbing Team
research.h1Example: Plumber in Culver City — 24/7 Emergency Service
research.metaDescPattern: Service + city + differentiator (response time, licensing, warranty)
research.metaDescExample: Licensed plumber in Culver City, CA. 24/7 emergency service, upfront pricing, satisfaction guaranteed. Call (310) 555-0100.
voiceoverHooks: Review velocity gap (recent 90 days — heaviest weighted in 2026)
Days since last review (>90 = dormant signal)
Stale GBP posts (top performers post weekly)
NAP mismatch on website (call-tracker number differing from GBP)
No service-area pages for multi-city operators
voiceoverAntipatterns: DO NOT advise switching to a narrower category — 'Plumber' is canonical.
DO NOT cite lifetime review counts without context — 2026 algorithm weighs recent 90 days heaviest.
clientOnboardingChecklist: Add all recommended secondary categories where services match
Set complete hours including 24/7 emergency window if offered
Write GBP description: 150-750 chars with service + city + differentiator
Upload 20+ photos (vans, team, completed work, before/after)
Set up automated SMS review request post-job (Podium/Birdeye/GatherUp)
Build citation profile across 14 targets, verify NAP exact match
Add LocalBusiness schema with full NAP + service area + hours
Set up review response process — target ≥80% response rate
Build service-area landing pages per city served (min 3)
Audit website: HTTPS, schema, <2.5s load, NAP above fold, single H1

kpiTargets.rankingTimeline: Map Pack 30-60 days; meaningful lead lift 3-5 months

industryNotes: Proximity is the single biggest factor (~55%) — operators outside the city center lose Map Pack even with strong reviews.
Google now weighs the last 90 days of reviews heaviest. 'Steady weekly flow' beats 'large historical total'.
GBP signals account for 32% — completing every field is the cheapest big lever.
Emergency/24/7 attributes meaningfully lift conversion AND ranking for emergency-intent queries.
consumesIn: FUTURE: Brain client-strategy module for plumber clients
FUTURE: admin onboarding wizard with recommended categories
FUTURE: portal KPI dashboard with kpiTargets thresholds
